mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods found in this batch. This batch contains Resque job helpers, shipment tracking helpers, and plan update helpers, but no printer enrollment or subscription setup utilities. Enrollment methods may exist in other batches containing printer management or subscription setup modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription state verification methods found in this batch. RABaseHelper has general data extraction methods but no subscription-specific verification. Subscription verification utilities may exist in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. Subscription status checking utilities may exist in other batches containing subscription management modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No plan pause methods found in this batch. UpdatePlanHelper has cancellation_subscription method but no pause plan functionality. Plan pause methods may exist in other batches containing plan management page objects or helpers.

matched_page_objects.class_purpose: Base class for all page objects in the framework providing the core locator management pattern. Stores the Playwright Page instance, creates an ElementLocators instance, and creates a WaitProxy instance. Implements __getattr__ magic method to provide dynamic attribute access - when an attribute is accessed that matches a selector name in the elements object, it automatically returns a Playwright locator object for that selector. Provides get_selector method to retrieve raw selector strings. This architecture allows page objects to define selectors as simple string attributes and access them as Playwright locators without manual locator creation code.
matched_page_objects.relevance_reason: This is the base class that the PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object would extend. It provides the foundational infrastructure for locator management and dynamic attribute access. While not directly used in test steps, it is the architectural foundation that enables page object creation. The test case requires a PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object which would inherit from this base class to gain locator management capabilities.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking a navigation link with the provided page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the page_name parameter. The XPath pattern is '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]'. The normalize-space function removes leading and trailing whitespace from the link text for more reliable matching. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is commonly used to navigate through the application's main navigation menu.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 require navigating to the Print and Payment History page. This method can click navigation links by text, which could be used to navigate to the target page if there is a navigation link with text 'Print and Payment History' or similar. However, the test case suggests direct URL navigation may be more appropriate. This method is relevant as a potential navigation approach.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Locates and returns a card body element by searching for a card with a specific title in its header. Constructs a combined CSS selector that matches a div.card element containing either an h5.card-header with exact text matching the title or an h5.card-header with an anchor child whose text matches the title. The selector pattern is 'div.card:has(h5.card-header:text-is("{title}"), h5.card-header a:text-is("{title}"))'. First attempts to locate the card using page.locator(). If the card count is 0, waits up to 10 seconds for the card to appear. If still not found after waiting, raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the field with that title was not found. If the card is found, returns a locator for the card body element within that card using card.locator('div.card-body'). This method is commonly used to extract data from labeled sections on detail pages.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Multiple steps require locating and verifying the 'Billing Cycle Period' card and its contents. This method can locate a card by its title text, which matches the test case requirement to 'Check the Billing Cycle Period card'. The method returns the card body element which can then be used to locate child elements like progress bars, text values, and info messages. This is highly relevant for steps that verify card contents.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then calls inner_text() on the field element to extract all visible text content including nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is commonly used to extract data values from labeled fields on detail pages for validation in test assertions.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 8, 9, and 19 require extracting text values from sections within the Billing Cycle Period card. Step 8 needs to verify 'Complimentary pages' value text, step 9 needs to verify the info message below 'Complimentary pages', and step 19 needs to verify total pages printed text. This method can extract text from titled sections, which could be useful if the page structure uses card-based layouts with titles.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will throw a TimeoutError. This is commonly used after navigation to ensure the target page has fully loaded before proceeding with further interactions.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 involve navigating to the Print and Payment History page and need to verify the page loaded successfully. This method waits for a page to load by checking for an h1 heading with specific text. If the Print and Payment History page has an h1 heading with 'Details for Print and Payment History' or similar, this method could be used to verify page load. However, the selector pattern is specific to 'Details for' prefix which may not match the actual page heading.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with Resque job scheduler, managing background job execution, navigating application pages and menus, extracting data from page elements by title, accessing links within titled sections, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. This helper class encapsulates common patterns for Resque job orchestration and page element interaction that are reused across multiple test scenarios. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper class provides utility methods for page navigation and card-based data extraction. The get_item_by_title method is particularly relevant for locating the Billing Cycle Period card and extracting data from it. The access_page method could be used for navigation. While this helper is designed for a specific application architecture with card-based layouts, its methods could be adapted for the Print and Payment History page if it follows similar patterns.
